bugs.title: Entry delete UI skipped child cascade for photos/documents
Pull scope dropped deleted parent IDs and blocked child tombstones
Project delete local cascade missed project_assignments
Project restore did not restore remote scope or child tombstones until project restore RPC and local restore cascade were added
Pull cursors did not advance past already-matching remote rows fetched inside the safety window
support_tickets local SQLite schema missed updated_at after the remote table gained it, causing a repeat-pull conflict loop

Shared local-purge delete contract for soft-delete tables was unsafe: once the sender physically removed its local row, push escalated to remote physical delete and lagging receivers could miss the tombstone entirely
Local hard purge now preserves tombstone metadata for soft-delete tables and push replays the preserved soft-delete instead of remote-hard-deleting when the local row is gone
Maintenance-assisted orphan purge now cleans stale synced_projects enrollment in the same housekeeping cycle
support_tickets.updated_at is functionally fixed for sync convergence, but upgraded SQLite installs still drift from the canonical NOT NULL DEFAULT schema and log startup/integrity warnings
S21 /projects can keep rendering the hard-deleted project card immediately after local purge even when the underlying SQLite row is gone
Windows /projects did not immediately surface the fresh assigned project card even when local SQLite, project_assignments, and synced_projects were all materialized

Project assignment mutation ownership was wrong: the assignment wizard/provider could write pull-only project_assignments locally, leaving exhausted change_log retries and RLS-denied residue on device
Legacy exhausted project_assignments change_log retries from the pre-fix contract breach still remain on upgraded devices and need cleanup or explicit repair handling
removeFromDevice could race the shared sync engine mutex, allowing a local-only scope eviction to overlap pull/push and leave a partially rematerialized project state
removeFromDevice preserved the project shell but left stale pull cursors behind, so a later fresh pull could miss older remote rows and fail to fully rematerialize the local scope
driver /inject-document-direct still advertises csv-capable fixture input while DocumentRepository correctly rejects csv documents; the harness should align with the shipped document allowlist

Driving the Help & Support success-screen Done action can trigger SupportProvider.reset while the framework is locked, destabilizing the Android driver process even though the underlying support ticket sync state is healthy
Consent copy says users can revoke consent in Settings, but the shipped Settings surface does not currently expose a revoke-consent control (tracked in GitHub issue #259)
After crash/relaunch, sync run-state reporting can go false-idle: /driver/sync-status reports isSyncing = false while /driver/sync still rejects new work as another sync is already in progress
Switching realtime hint subscriptions to private channels initially broke client authorization because the hosted project lacked a realtime.messages SELECT policy for the opaque sync_hint topics

Foreground private sync_hint delivery was nondeterministic because the branch relied on opaque remote trigger side effects; push now emits the owned emit_sync_hint RPC directly and active channel lookup no longer depends on user_profiles joins
driver /inject-photo-direct returned a harness-side 500 for one tiny-PNG soak attempt even though the shipped file-backed sync lane is already proven through normal flows

observations: Remote project delete RPC correctly tombstones full subtree including project_assignments.
Project delete propagation verifier project snapshots are now deduplicated before the next live delete proof wave.
Both devices now explicitly prove the deleted project and deleted entry are absent from the active list UIs.
Remote storage verification for the deleted project returned zero matches for all surviving local photo/document remote paths.

Project restore now revives the sender subtree locally, restores remote rows for projects/project_assignments/daily_entries/photos/documents, rehydrates the receiver SQLite subtree after pull, and renders in both devices' project/entry UIs.
After the pull cursor fix and support_tickets updated_at schema alignment, repeated full sync runs on both Windows and S21 settle at pushed=0, pulled=0.
Project hard delete currently produces immediate sender-local purge, but receiver cleanup depended on a forced integrity/orphan-purge cycle rather than ordinary pull convergence.

Audit result: the underlying risk is broader than Trash. Any client-side local purge on a soft-delete sync table that leaves behind a delete change must preserve a last tombstone payload instead of escalating to remote physical delete.

Patch result: local hard purge now preserves tombstone metadata in change_log, push replays that preserved soft-delete when the local row is already gone, and maintenance now prunes stale synced_projects immediately after orphan purge.
The support_tickets updated_at sync defect is fixed functionally, but upgraded SQLite files still log schema drift because migration v53 adds the column without the canonical NOT NULL DEFAULT shape.

Fresh-fixture hard-delete revalidation proved the new contract: sender Delete Forever now locally purges the sender shell, preserves and re-timestamps the remote soft tombstone, and receivers remain on a hidden tombstone state instead of losing the delete entirely.
After the fresh-fixture hard delete, both devices settled immediately on repeated sync at pushed=0, pulled=0 with no pending project delete changes.
Plain assignment revocation of an active project now fully evicts the revoked Windows cache while the remote project stays active for S21.

When project delete tombstones and assignment revocation arrive together, both devices currently retain a hidden project tombstone with synced_projects unenrolled instead of fully evicting the local row.

Project assignment mutation is now remote-first again: the mutation owner diffs against active remote assignments only, restores through a sanctioned RPC path, and no new local project_assignments change_log residue is created during the live assignment-save flow.
Upgraded-install support_tickets drift is now closed live: S21 migration v55 rebuilt the table and startup SchemaVerifier now reports drift=0.
Legacy project_assignments retry residue is now closed live: S21 migration v56 purged the two invalid pending rows and /driver/change-log?table=project_assignments now returns count=0.
Local-only remove-from-device is now serialized behind the same SQLite sync mutex as foreground/background sync, eliminating the race where local scope eviction could overlap an in-flight sync cycle.

Local-only remove-from-device now clears project-scoped pull cursors when it preserves the project metadata shell, so the next healthy cycle performs a true fresh pull instead of relying on stale table cursors.

Windows fresh-pull parity is now live-proven on project e7dde2a2-8662-4d5a-ad32-3e167ad5576d: local scope dropped to a preserved shell, S21 stayed active, the next Windows sync rematerialized the full subtree, and a second Windows sync settled at pushed=0, pulled=0.

S21 fresh-pull parity is now live-proven on the same project: local scope dropped to a preserved shell, Windows stayed active, the next healthy S21 sync rematerialized the full subtree and both active assignments, and the next consecutive S21 sync settled at pushed=0, pulled=0.

File-backed live proof is now closed on shared fixture entry 4e35a00d-26d7-44b7-9f5d-c67c9e6f2f91: documents, photos, entry_exports, and form_exports all push from S21, materialize on Windows, settle at pushed=0/pulled=0 on repeat sync, tombstone through the shipped entry-delete path, and converge on the same deleted subtree on both devices.

Authenticated Supabase REST checks now explicitly prove the file-backed wave on both sides: the create half produced live remote rows plus 200-accessible storage objects, and the delete half produced remote tombstones plus storage not_found responses for all four exact object paths.
Forced integrity rerun is now live-proven on both devices: after resetting last_integrity_check, both Windows and S21 completed full maintenance cycles at pushed=0, pulled=0.

Maintenance orphan purge is now directly proven on pay-app/file-backed tables: a local-only Windows export_artifacts/pay_applications orphan pair missing remotely was detected by integrity, marked cursor_reset_recommended, then soft-deleted locally as system_orphan_purge with no new change_log residue.
After the maintenance-assisted orphan purge, repeated full sync on both Windows and S21 again settled immediately at pushed=0, pulled=0, so the cleanup does not leave recurring drift.

The current sync registry had outpaced the remote schema: support-ticket proof surfaced missing Supabase tables signature_files and signature_audit_log, and applying supabase/migrations/20260408000000_signature_tables.sql closed that branch-level blocker live.

The dedicated support-ticket live flow is now proven on its correct user-scoped contract: S21 Help & Support UI inserted ticket 833518ea-f187-4c54-ba6b-bce95f1a3e0e, Supabase now stores the row, and S21 repeat sync returned to pushed=0, pulled=0 with no pending support_tickets residue.
Protected sync tables are no longer generically mutable through DriverServer create/update endpoints, and custom lint now guards that harness boundary against regression.

Consent is now live-proven through the real router/version gate on both device identities: stale stored policy versions forced `/consent`, the shipped accept action appended new local rows, Supabase stored the same new accepted records, and repeat sync returned to pushed=0, pulled=0.

Kill-during-push is now live-proven on a real file-backed lane: after force-stopping S21 mid-sync with six pending photo inserts, the next healthy relaunch replayed the durable change_log work, materialized exactly one remote row per photo, Windows pulled the same six IDs, and repeated sync returned to pushed=0, pulled=0 on both devices.

The retry/restart push wave also exposed a remaining architecture gap: delayed background retry scheduling is still timer-memory only, while durable recovery currently depends on persisted work queues such as change_log and storage_cleanup_queue rather than a separate failed_sync_queue.

Kill-during-pull is now live-proven on a multi-page file-backed batch: Windows was killed after partially materializing 55 of 120 new retry_pullb photos, the photos pull cursor stayed on the old pre-batch value, and the next ordinary sync after relaunch pulled the missing 65 without duplication or remote loss.

Delete propagation and storage cleanup are now both live-proven twice: once on fixtures 96af93cc-fb77-4bed-9006-3ecae4efc426 / 6899b31c-e884-4cf6-9c95-983080d44af7 and again on fresh fixtures db3b76ff-f749-415b-b21b-c49853aebbcb / 0c2eba96-1c92-4e88-a0b6-ca9501fc04e8.

Across the second retry/restart sprint, the data plane stayed durable again: retry_push2 recovered six photo inserts, retry_pullc recovered a 94/120 partial Windows pull, and both fresh delete/cleanup subtrees converged without duplication or data loss.

The stale crash-left run-state defect is now closed live: seeding a Windows sync_lock + pulling=1 state, relaunching the real app, and checking the shared gate showed startup recovery cleared the lock, sync-status reported idle truthfully, and an immediate sync was admitted successfully.

Quick-resume catch-up is now live-proven on shared entry_quantities row f369778b-4d4d-4fd5-a1c4-613540ee5724: the row was absent while S21 stayed backgrounded, then materialized locally after foreground resume without any manual S21 sync call.

During the quick-resume proof, persisted last_sync_time did not advance even though the resumed device materialized the new shared row, so the next realtime-hint / mode diagnostics pass should verify whether this is an intentional hint-driven catch-up path or a timestamp-writeback gap.

Realtime-hint auth is now fixed live: once the hosted project gained a realtime.messages SELECT policy keyed to sync_hint_subscriptions.channel_name = realtime.topic(), both Windows and S21 returned transportHealth=active/subscribed for their private sync_hint:* channels instead of Unauthorized.

Realtime-hint delivery is now live-proven again, but through a more deterministic contract: the sender push path emits emit_sync_hint(...) explicitly after successful remote writes, and the active-channel lookup now uses sync_hint_subscriptions directly instead of re-joining user_profiles.

The decisive foreground proof row is 680adba1-50ac-47ee-894b-251ecd059f4f: Windows pushed it successfully, S21 stayed foregrounded and subscribed, S21 transport recorded a fresh quick run, and S21 materialized the row without manual sync.

Global full sync is now live-proven on both devices with no-hint remote project renames against shared project e7dde2a2-8662-4d5a-ad32-3e167ad5576d. Windows and S21 each pulled the remote rename in full mode with dirty scopes empty beforehand.

Dirty-scope isolation is now live-proven after RealtimeHintHandler learned to normalize nested private-broadcast envelopes: S21 marked shared project e7dde2a2-8662-4d5a-ad32-3e167ad5576d dirty for probe e3d86498-88dc-49e7-9406-9472de84a2fa, quick-synced the scoped quantity, and correctly ignored unrelated remote drift on project 4825141a-7b6b-44f9-9ef1-ba5e89dc39fd.

Private-channel lifecycle is now live-proven end to end: Windows sign-out revoked row a3a1c799-e8f3-4d4b-9a89-84348f0d3738, the next sign-in created active row dea887bf-911e-4b15-a0a3-3f8079180db7 on the same device_install_id, and post-rebind probe ed816553-474a-40a9-880a-e11d85884110 auto-materialized without manual sync.

The final mixed-flow soak passed across both devices: create on S21, edit on Windows, corrected tombstone on S21, remove-from-device and fresh full pull on Windows, then integrity + repeat sync all converged cleanly with no deleted-row resurrection.

The deliberate bad tombstone payload deleted_by = soak_s21 failed loudly with Postgres 22P02 invalid UUID instead of creating silent drift; after correction to the real S21 user UUID, the same row converged normally.

The release-proof sweep is now complete. No open high-severity sync blocker remains across lifecycle, file-backed, maintenance, insert-only, retry/restart, mode, channel-lifecycle, or mixed-flow validation lanes.
